server.name: kubernetes
server.description: Connect to a Kubernetes cluster and manage it.
server.title: Kubernetes

tools.name: cleanup
tools.description: Cleanup all managed resources

tools.name: exec_in_pod
tools.description: Execute a command in a Kubernetes pod or container and return the output. Command must be an array of strings where the first element is the executable and remaining elements are arguments. This executes directly without shell interpretation for security.

arguments.name: command
arguments.desc: Command to execute as an array of strings (e.g. ["ls", "-la", "/app"]). First element is the executable, remaining are arguments. Shell operators like pipes, redirects, or command chaining are not supported - use explicit array format for security.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the pod to execute the command in

arguments.name: container
arguments.desc: Container name (required when pod has multiple containers)

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: timeout
arguments.desc: Timeout for command - 60000 milliseconds if not specified

tools.name: explain_resource
tools.description: Get documentation for a Kubernetes resource or field

arguments.name: resource
arguments.desc: Resource name or field path (e.g. 'pods' or 'pods.spec.containers')

arguments.name: apiVersion
arguments.desc: API version to use (e.g. 'apps/v1')

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: output
arguments.desc: Output format (plaintext or plaintext-openapiv2)

arguments.name: recursive
arguments.desc: Print the fields of fields recursively

tools.name: install_helm_chart
tools.description: Install a Helm chart with support for both standard and template-based installation

arguments.name: chart
arguments.desc: Chart name (e.g., 'nginx') or path to chart directory

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the Helm release

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: createNamespace
arguments.desc: Create namespace if it doesn't exist

arguments.name: repo
arguments.desc: Helm repository URL (optional if using local chart path)

arguments.name: useTemplate
arguments.desc: Use helm template + kubectl apply instead of helm install (bypasses auth issues)

arguments.name: values
arguments.desc: Custom values to override chart defaults

arguments.name: valuesFile
arguments.desc: Path to values file (alternative to values object)

tools.name: kubectl_apply
tools.description: Apply a Kubernetes YAML manifest from a string or file

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: dryRun
arguments.desc: If true, only validate the resource, don't actually execute the operation

arguments.name: filename
arguments.desc: Path to a YAML file to apply (optional - use either manifest or filename)

arguments.name: force
arguments.desc: If true, immediately remove resources from API and bypass graceful deletion

arguments.name: manifest
arguments.desc: YAML manifest to apply

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

tools.name: kubectl_context
tools.description: Manage Kubernetes contexts - list, get, or set the current context

arguments.name: operation
arguments.desc: Operation to perform: list contexts, get current context, or set current context

arguments.name: detailed
arguments.desc: Include detailed information about the context

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the context to set as current (required for set operation)

arguments.name: output
arguments.desc: Output format

arguments.name: showCurrent
arguments.desc: When listing contexts, highlight which one is currently active

tools.name: kubectl_create
tools.description: Create Kubernetes resources using various methods (from file or using subcommands)

arguments.name: annotations
arguments.desc: Annotations to apply to the resource (e.g. ["key1=value1", "key2=value2"])

arguments.name: command
arguments.desc: Command to run in the container

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: dryRun
arguments.desc: If true, only validate the resource, don't actually execute the operation

arguments.name: filename
arguments.desc: Path to a YAML file to create resources from

arguments.name: fromFile
arguments.desc: Path to file for creating configmap (e.g. ["key1=/path/to/file1", "key2=/path/to/file2"])

arguments.name: fromLiteral
arguments.desc: Key-value pair for creating configmap (e.g. ["key1=value1", "key2=value2"])

arguments.name: image
arguments.desc: Image to use for the containers in the deployment

arguments.name: labels
arguments.desc: Labels to apply to the resource (e.g. ["key1=value1", "key2=value2"])

arguments.name: manifest
arguments.desc: YAML manifest to create resources from

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the resource to create

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: output
arguments.desc: Output format. One of: json|yaml|name|go-template|go-template-file|template|templatefile|jsonpath|jsonpath-as-json|jsonpath-file

arguments.name: port
arguments.desc: Port that the container exposes

arguments.name: replicas
arguments.desc: Number of replicas to create for the deployment

arguments.name: resourceType
arguments.desc: Type of resource to create (namespace, configmap, deployment, service, etc.)

arguments.name: schedule
arguments.desc: Cron schedule expression for the CronJob (e.g. "*/5 * * * *")

arguments.name: secretType
arguments.desc: Type of secret to create (generic, docker-registry, tls)

arguments.name: serviceType
arguments.desc: Type of service to create (clusterip, nodeport, loadbalancer, externalname)

arguments.name: suspend
arguments.desc: Whether to suspend the CronJob

arguments.name: tcpPort
arguments.desc: Port pairs for tcp service (e.g. ["80:8080", "443:8443"])

arguments.name: validate
arguments.desc: If true, validate resource schema against server schema

tools.name: kubectl_delete
tools.description: Delete Kubernetes resources by resource type, name, labels, or from a manifest file

arguments.name: allNamespaces
arguments.desc: If true, delete resources across all namespaces

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: filename
arguments.desc: Path to a YAML file to delete resources from (optional)

arguments.name: force
arguments.desc: If true, immediately remove resources from API and bypass graceful deletion

arguments.name: gracePeriodSeconds
arguments.desc: Period of time in seconds given to the resource to terminate gracefully

arguments.name: labelSelector
arguments.desc: Delete resources matching this label selector (e.g. 'app=nginx')

arguments.name: manifest
arguments.desc: YAML manifest defining resources to delete (optional)

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the resource to delete

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: resourceType
arguments.desc: Type of resource to delete (e.g., pods, deployments, services, etc.)

tools.name: kubectl_describe
tools.description: Describe Kubernetes resources by resource type, name, and optionally namespace

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the resource to describe

arguments.name: resourceType
arguments.desc: Type of resource to describe (e.g., pods, deployments, services, etc.)

arguments.name: allNamespaces
arguments.desc: If true, describe resources across all namespaces

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

tools.name: kubectl_generic
tools.description: Execute any kubectl command with the provided arguments and flags

arguments.name: command
arguments.desc: The kubectl command to execute (e.g. patch, rollout, top)

arguments.name: args
arguments.desc: Additional command arguments

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: flags
arguments.desc: Command flags as key-value pairs

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Resource name

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: outputFormat
arguments.desc: Output format (e.g. json, yaml, wide)

arguments.name: resourceType
arguments.desc: Resource type (e.g. pod, deployment)

arguments.name: subCommand
arguments.desc: Subcommand if applicable (e.g. 'history' for rollout)

tools.name: kubectl_get
tools.description: Get or list Kubernetes resources by resource type, name, and optionally namespace

arguments.name: resourceType
arguments.desc: Type of resource to get (e.g., pods, deployments, services, configmaps, events, etc.)

arguments.name: allNamespaces
arguments.desc: If true, list resources across all namespaces

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: fieldSelector
arguments.desc: Filter resources by field selector (e.g. 'metadata.name=my-pod')

arguments.name: labelSelector
arguments.desc: Filter resources by label selector (e.g. 'app=nginx')

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the resource (optional - if not provided, lists all resources of the specified type)

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: output
arguments.desc: Output format

arguments.name: sortBy
arguments.desc: Sort events by a field (default: lastTimestamp). Only applicable for events.

tools.name: kubectl_logs
tools.description: Get logs from Kubernetes resources like pods, deployments, or jobs

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the resource

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: resourceType
arguments.desc: Type of resource to get logs from

arguments.name: container
arguments.desc: Container name (required when pod has multiple containers)

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: follow
arguments.desc: Follow logs output (not recommended, may cause timeouts)

arguments.name: labelSelector
arguments.desc: Filter resources by label selector

arguments.name: previous
arguments.desc: Include logs from previously terminated containers

arguments.name: since
arguments.desc: Show logs since relative time (e.g. '5s', '2m', '3h')

arguments.name: sinceTime
arguments.desc: Show logs since absolute time (RFC3339)

arguments.name: tail
arguments.desc: Number of lines to show from end of logs

arguments.name: timestamps
arguments.desc: Include timestamps in logs

tools.name: kubectl_patch
tools.description: Update field(s) of a resource using strategic merge patch, JSON merge patch, or JSON patch

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the resource to patch

arguments.name: resourceType
arguments.desc: Type of resource to patch (e.g., pods, deployments, services)

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: dryRun
arguments.desc: If true, only validate the resource, don't actually execute the operation

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: patchData
arguments.desc: Patch data as a JSON object

arguments.name: patchFile
arguments.desc: Path to a file containing the patch data (alternative to patchData)

arguments.name: patchType
arguments.desc: Type of patch to apply

tools.name: kubectl_reconnect
tools.description: Reconnect to the Kubernetes API server by recreating all API clients. Use this after cluster upgrades (e.g., EKS control plane upgrades that rotate ENIs/IPs) to force fresh DNS resolution and new TCP connections.

tools.name: kubectl_rollout
tools.description: Manage the rollout of a resource (e.g., deployment, daemonset, statefulset)

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the resource

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: resourceType
arguments.desc: Type of resource to manage rollout for

arguments.name: subCommand
arguments.desc: Rollout subcommand to execute

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: revision
arguments.desc: Revision to rollback to (for undo subcommand)

arguments.name: timeout
arguments.desc: The length of time to wait before giving up (e.g., '30s', '1m', '2m30s')

arguments.name: toRevision
arguments.desc: Revision to roll back to (for history subcommand)

arguments.name: watch
arguments.desc: Watch the rollout status in real-time until completion

tools.name: kubectl_scale
tools.description: Scale a Kubernetes deployment

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the deployment to scale

arguments.name: replicas
arguments.desc: Number of replicas to scale to

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: resourceType
arguments.desc: Resource type to scale (deployment, replicaset, statefulset)

tools.name: list_api_resources
tools.description: List the API resources available in the cluster

arguments.name: apiGroup
arguments.desc: API group to filter by

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: namespaced
arguments.desc: If true, only show namespaced resources

arguments.name: output
arguments.desc: Output format (wide, name, or no-headers)

arguments.name: verbs
arguments.desc: List of verbs to filter by

tools.name: node_management
tools.description: Manage Kubernetes nodes with cordon, drain, and uncordon operations

arguments.name: operation
arguments.desc: Node operation to perform

arguments.name: confirmDrain
arguments.desc: Explicit confirmation to drain the node (required for drain operation)

arguments.name: deleteLocalData
arguments.desc: Delete local data even if emptyDir volumes are used (for drain operation)

arguments.name: dryRun
arguments.desc: Show what would be done without actually doing it (for drain operation)

arguments.name: force
arguments.desc: Force the operation even if there are pods not managed by a ReplicationController, ReplicaSet, Job, DaemonSet or StatefulSet (for drain operation)

arguments.name: gracePeriod
arguments.desc: Period of time in seconds given to each pod to terminate gracefully (for drain operation). If set to -1, uses the kubectl default grace period.

arguments.name: ignoreDaemonsets
arguments.desc: Ignore DaemonSet-managed pods (for drain operation)

arguments.name: nodeName
arguments.desc: Name of the node to operate on (required for cordon, drain, uncordon)

arguments.name: timeout
arguments.desc: The length of time to wait before giving up (for drain operation, e.g., '5m', '1h')

tools.name: ping
tools.description: Verify that the counterpart is still responsive and the connection is alive.

tools.name: port_forward
tools.description: Forward a local port to a port on a Kubernetes resource

tools.name: stop_port_forward
tools.description: Stop a port-forward process

tools.name: uninstall_helm_chart
tools.description: Uninstall a Helm chart release

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the Helm release to uninstall

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

tools.name: upgrade_helm_chart
tools.description: Upgrade an existing Helm chart release

arguments.name: chart
arguments.desc: Chart name or path to chart directory

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: Name of the Helm release to upgrade

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: Kubernetes namespace

arguments.name: context
arguments.desc: Kubeconfig Context to use for the command (optional - defaults to null)

arguments.name: repo
arguments.desc: Helm repository URL (optional if using local chart path)

arguments.name: values
arguments.desc: Custom values to override chart defaults

arguments.name: valuesFile
arguments.desc: Path to values file (alternative to values object)

config.description: Configure the location of the host Kubernetes file
config.name: kubernetes
